summary: Obfuscated remote-code dropper executed on require()
details: The package's main file index.js is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io RC4-encoded string array with hex-named identifiers hiding all imports, URLs, and command strings). On require(), top-level code loads child_process, fs, os, path, and an HTTP client, immediately invokes child_process.exec on a constructed command, performs an HTTP GET to a remote URL, writes the response body to a file under os.tmpdir(), and then executes that downloaded file via child_process.exec with {windowsHide:true, cwd:process.cwd()} — a classic fetch-and-execute dropper that runs attacker-controlled bytes on the installer's machine the moment any project imports the module. Package metadata reinforces the malicious intent: empty description and author, no declared dependencies (despite the runtime HTTP client requirement), and a README that instructs `npm install nodepull` and `require('nodepull')` while the published name is `@array-util/nodepull` — a drive-by traffic ploy targeting a name resembling generic node/array utility libraries.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
